exposure.description: Use graduated adjustments: darken sky 0.5-1.0 stops, lift shadows in foreground. Recover highlights first, then lift shadows. Never clip blacks.

color.description: Boost vibrance 10-20% (not saturation). Split tone: warm highlights (orange +5), cool shadows (blue +5). Green channel boost for foliage.

sharpening.description: Apply to midtones only. Amount 40-60, radius 1.0. Avoid sharpening sky areas.

noise_reduction.description: Apply selectively to sky and shadow areas only. Preserve texture in foliage and rock detail.

common_mistakes: Over-saturated skies turn neon blue -- Shutterstock rejects unnatural colors
Crushed blacks lose shadow detail needed for commercial use
HDR halo around horizon line is an instant rejection
Over-sharpened clouds create visible artifacts

exposure.description: Expose for skin tones. Lift shadows on face, darken busy backgrounds. Use radial mask around face to increase exposure +0.3 stops.

color.description: Warm skin tones slightly. Reduce orange saturation to avoid fake tan look. Keep white balance neutral-warm.

sharpening.description: Sharpen eyes and hair only. Reduce clarity on skin for slight smoothing. Amount 30-40 max.

noise_reduction.description: Gentle NR on skin only. Over-NR creates plastic/waxy look that Shutterstock rejects. Keep pores and texture visible.

common_mistakes: Plastic skin from over-smoothing -- stock reviewers inspect at 100%
Orange/magenta color cast from white balance errors
Sharpening halos around hair edges
Over-whitened teeth or eyes look unnatural

exposure.description: Bright and inviting. Lift overall exposure slightly. Brighten highlights on glossy surfaces. Darken background for mood.

color.description: Boost warm tones (orange, yellow, red) by 10-15%. Desaturate blues and greens slightly for appetizing look. Increase vibrance.

sharpening.description: Moderate sharpening to bring out texture in food. Amount 50-60, radius 1.2. Show texture of bread crusts, cheese, herbs.

noise_reduction.description: Minimal NR. Food photos should retain natural texture. Only reduce noise if shot at high ISO.

common_mistakes: Over-saturated reds make food look artificial
Blue/cool color cast makes food look unappetizing
Too much clarity on steam/smoke creates halos
Flat lighting with no contrast looks amateur

exposure.description: Balanced exposure showing interior and exterior detail. Recover highlights in windows. Lift shadows in structural details.

color.description: Neutral and accurate colors. Correct white balance for mixed lighting. Slight desaturation for clean modern look.

sharpening.description: Strong sharpening on edges and lines. Amount 60-70, radius 1.0. Architectural detail should be crisp.

noise_reduction.description: Moderate NR especially on large flat surfaces (walls, sky). Preserve edge detail on building lines.

common_mistakes: Converging verticals -- correct perspective distortion
Mixed white balance (daylight + tungsten) creates color zones
Over-sharpened brick/stone texture looks crunchy
Noise in large sky areas behind buildings

exposure.description: Slightly contrasty for mood. Darken shadows for depth, keep highlights alive. Embrace natural light.

color.description: Slight desaturation for documentary feel. Warm highlights, cool shadows split tone. Keep it natural.

sharpening.description: Moderate sharpening. Amount 45-55. Street photos need detail but not over-processed look.

noise_reduction.description: Light NR only. Some grain adds character to street photography but Shutterstock prefers clean images.

common_mistakes: Heavy vignette looks dated and amateur
Over-processed HDR ruins natural street atmosphere
Identifiable faces without model releases will be rejected
Tilted horizons are an instant reject for stock

exposure.description: Expose for the subject. Brighten animal/bird subject, darken distracting background. Recover detail in fur/feathers.

color.description: Boost vibrance for natural colors. Enhance greens for foliage. Keep animal colors accurate -- no oversaturation.

sharpening.description: Careful sharpening on eyes and feathers/fur. Amount 50-60, radius 0.8. Eyes must be tack sharp for stock acceptance.

noise_reduction.description: High ISO common in wildlife. Apply moderate NR to out-of-focus areas, preserve detail on subject.

common_mistakes: Soft eyes on the animal -- must be critically sharp
Noise in bokeh/background from high ISO
Over-saturated greens look fake
Cropping too tight loses commercial usability

exposure.description: High contrast for maximum visual impact. Darken shadows aggressively, boost highlights. Create drama.

color.description: Bold, saturated colors work well. Boost vibrance 15-25%. Complementary color schemes (orange/blue, red/green).

sharpening.description: Strong sharpening to bring out texture detail. Amount 60-80, radius 1.2. Texture is the whole point.

noise_reduction.description: Minimal NR. Texture patterns should remain sharp and detailed.

common_mistakes: Not enough contrast -- abstracts need visual punch
Leaving dust spots that blend with the pattern
Uneven lighting creating hot spots
Image too busy with no clear focal point

exposure.description: Clean, bright, professional. Slight overexposure (+0.3) for airy commercial feel. Even lighting across frame.

color.description: Clean neutral colors. Slight warmth for approachable feel. White backgrounds should be pure white.

sharpening.description: Moderate, clean sharpening. Amount 40-50, radius 1.0. Professional but not over-processed.

noise_reduction.description: Clean images expected. Apply NR to any noise present. Studio/office environments should be grain-free.

common_mistakes: Visible logos or brand names will be rejected without property release
Uneven skin tones from mixed lighting
Cluttered background reduces commercial value
Not enough negative space for text placement

exposure: Check histogram -- avoid clipping in highlights or shadows
Recover blown highlights before other adjustments
Lift shadows conservatively -- excessive shadow recovery adds noise
sharpening: Never sharpen above amount 80 -- creates visible halos
Apply capture sharpening, not creative sharpening
Always check for halos at 100% zoom after sharpening
noise_reduction: Selective NR over global -- preserve texture where it matters
Keep texture in hair, fabric, stone, bark, feathers
Smooth sky, out-of-focus areas, and uniform surfaces
Over-NR creates plastic/waxy look -- Shutterstock rejects this
Check ISO in EXIF: < 400 rarely needs NR, 800-1600 moderate, > 3200 heavy
color: sRGB color profile mandatory for Shutterstock
White balance should be neutral for stock -- avoid extreme casts
Boost vibrance over saturation for natural look
Check skin tones look natural -- no orange or magenta casts
composition: Straighten horizon lines -- tilted horizons are rejected
Leave negative space for text placement (increases commercial value)
Remove distracting elements at edges of frame
cleanup: Remove all dust spots, sensor marks, lens flare
Fix chromatic aberration (purple/green fringing on high-contrast edges)
Remove any copyrighted logos, brand names, or identifiable text
Clean up distracting elements in background

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Visible noise/grain at 100% zoom, especially in shadows and sky gradients
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check patches from shadow areas and smooth gradients

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Light or dark halo along high-contrast edges from over-sharpening
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check edge contrast ratio at 100% zoom

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Plastic/waxy appearance from excessive noise reduction, loss of fine detail
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check texture detail retention in detailed areas

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Visible steps/bands in color gradients instead of smooth transitions
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check gradient areas for step artifacts

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Purple or green fringing along high-contrast edges
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check edges of dark objects against bright backgrounds

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Dark or light circular marks from sensor dust
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check uniform areas like sky at 100% zoom

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: Over-saturated, over-contrasted HDR look that appears unnatural
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check overall saturation levels and local contrast

shutterstock_rejection_triggers.description: JPEG macroblocking visible in smooth areas
shutterstock_rejection_triggers.detection: Check smooth gradient areas for blocky patterns

stock_ready_mode.description: Minimal, authentic edits optimized for stock photography acceptance. Less is more -- buyers want natural-looking images, not over-processed ones.
philosophy: Authenticity over perfection -- buyers reject overly polished/artificial images
Subtle corrections only -- fix technical issues, don't transform the mood
Preserve natural skin tones, lighting, and atmosphere
Clean but not sterile -- keep the human/organic feel
Ensure technical quality (sharp, well-exposed, low noise) without visible editing
rules: NO heavy color grading -- keep colors natural
NO vignette -- stock reviewers flag it
NO film grain -- must be clean
NO extreme contrast -- keep shadows and highlights recoverable
MINIMAL noise reduction -- preserve texture at all costs
ALWAYS remove dust spots and sensor marks

top_categories_by_sales.tip: Authentic over staged. Real-looking people in real settings outsell polished corporate stock.
Wellness lifestyle shots with natural lighting. Diverse representation is key.
Abstract tech concepts and real people interacting with technology both sell well.
Corporate sustainability reporting drives massive demand. Think annual reports.
Representation matters. Diverse images consistently outperform homogeneous ones.
Overhead and 45-degree angles. Natural light. Style with minimal props.
Generic sunsets are saturated. Stand out with unique angles, local culture, authentic travel moments.
Candid over posed. Real family moments in real homes.
E-learning and hybrid education imagery growing fast.
Upload 2-3 months before the holiday. Demand surges predictably.

shot_list_keywords.description: Shutterstock publishes monthly Shot List keywords. Include these magic keywords to get surfaced to active buyers.
